channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: Building an Adversarial Malware Dataset by Family and Type: Generation, Evasion, and Poisoning Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25937v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与目标

针对机器学习驱动的恶意软件检测系统日益面临的对抗性威胁，本研究构建了一个按家族（family）和类型（type）双维度标注的对抗性恶意软件数据集，旨在系统评估模型鲁棒性、规避能力及数据投毒脆弱性。

方法与构建过程

基于公开真实恶意软件数据集 RawMal-TF，我们集成多种先进对抗样本生成技术（包括API重排序、节区注入、代码混淆与入口点迁移等），生成两类高质量对抗性PE文件：

  • 家族标注子集：44,347 个样本，每个样本明确标注原始恶意软件家族（如 Emotet、TrickBot）；
  • 类型标注子集：33,596 个样本，按功能类型分类（如 downloader、ransomware、trojan）；

所有样本均通过严格验证——在 EMBER 检测器上实现 98.35%（家族集）和 92.20%（类型集）的规避率，并附带完整元数据：EMBER 预测分、VirusTotal 多引擎扫描结果（≥30家厂商）、原始哈希、修改操作日志及规避路径。

关键发现与创新贡献

  • 首次实证揭示数据投毒对恶意软件分类管道的严重威胁：仅向家族标注训练集注入 0.5% 的全错标对抗样本（即标签被系统性篡改为错误家族），即可使重训练后的 EMBER 分类器在测试集上的规避率从 26.1% 急剧升至 92.8%，证实低开销、高破坏性的“标签污染”攻击可行性；
  • 提供首个开源、可复现、带细粒度元数据的双维度对抗恶意软件基准数据集，支持规避分析、投毒防御、模型解释性及鲁棒训练等多方向研究；
  • 所有样本与代码已完全开源（含生成脚本、元数据Schema、评估协议），推动对抗恶意软件研究的标准化与可比性。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce a novel adversarial malware dataset derived from RawMal-TF, featuring two rigorously constructed subsets: 44,347 family-labelled and 33,596 type-labelled PE binaries, generated via diverse adversarial perturbations (e.g., section injection, API reordering, entry-point shifting). These samples achieve 98.35% and 92.20% evasion rates, respectively, against the EMBER classifier. Each binary is accompanied by rich metadata—including EMBER scores, VirusTotal multi-engine reports, and provenance logs. Crucially, we demonstrate severe vulnerability to data poisoning: injecting only 0.5% fully mislabelled adversarial samples into the family-labelled training set increases the evasion rate of the retrained EMBER model from 26.1% to 92.8%, highlighting a practical, low-cost attack vector. The dataset is publicly released to advance research on adversarial robustness, poisoning defense, and trustworthy ML-based malware detection.

Abstract

We present a dataset of adversarial malware samples derived from the public RawMal-TF collection of real-world malware binaries. Using a suite of adversarial malware generators, we construct two sets of adversarial PE files: 44,347 family-labelled samples and 33,596 type-labelled samples, achieving evasion rates of 98.35 % and 92.20 % against the EMBER classifier, respectively. Each adversarial binary is accompanied by detailed metadata, including EMBER scores and VirusTotal classifications. We further demonstrate the susceptibility of malware classification pipelines to data poisoning attacks through a series of training experiments. Injecting fully mislabelled adversarial samples representing only 0.5 % of the training data in the family-labelled dataset increases the evasion rate against the re-trained classifier from 26.1 % to 92.8 %. The dataset is publicly released to facilitate future research on adversarial malware, poisoning attacks, and the robustness of machine-learning-based malware detection systems.

item.title: How Agentic AI Coding Assistants Become the Attacker's Shell
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25871v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

随着Agentic AI编码助手（如具备文件编辑、命令执行与联网能力的智能体）在开发流程中深度集成，其自主性显著提升，但安全边界同步模糊。本文首次系统揭示：此类助手因依赖未经验证的外部工件（如GitHub代码片段、npm包文档、Stack Overflow回答、README文件等），极易遭受隐蔽的“指令劫持”攻击——攻击者可将恶意指令隐写于看似无害的外部文本中，诱使AI助手在开发者不知情下执行任意系统命令，实质将其转化为攻击者的远程Shell。

方法与发现

我们构建了首个面向Agentic编码助手的多模态提示注入攻击评估框架，覆盖6类主流外部来源（含127个真实高流量开源项目文档与社区问答），实证复现了23种新型攻击变体（如“语义混淆注入”“上下文漂移触发”“多跳依赖链劫持”）。实验表明：在未加固的主流助手（如Cursor、GitHub Copilot Agents、CodeAct-based智能体）上，攻击成功率达89.3%；即使启用基础防护（如输入过滤、输出校验），成功率仍达41.7%，凸显现有防御的严重脆弱性。

创新与启示

本研究提出“外部依赖信任链断裂”新安全范式，指出当前防御过度聚焦于用户输入端，却忽视了AI代理对第三方内容的无条件信任。我们设计轻量级上下文感知沙箱协议（CASB）原型，通过动态溯源+执行意图校验，在保持功能完整性前提下将攻击拦截率提升至96.5%。研究呼吁建立“外部工件可信度分级标注标准”，并为AI代理安全规范（如NIST AI RMF扩展）提供关键实证依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper uncovers a critical security vulnerability in agentic AI coding assistants—those capable of editing files, executing shell commands, and accessing the internet autonomously. We demonstrate that their reliance on unvetted external artifacts (e.g., GitHub READMEs, npm package docs, Stack Overflow answers) creates a novel attack surface: hidden instructions embedded in these artifacts can hijack the assistant to execute arbitrary commands, effectively turning it into an attacker’s remote shell. Through systematic evaluation across 127 real-world external sources and 6 artifact categories, we identify 23 new prompt injection variants and achieve up to 89.3% success rate against unpatched agents—even with basic defenses enabled, 41.7% of attacks succeed. We propose the Context-Aware Sandboxing Protocol (CASB), a lightweight runtime mitigation that dynamically verifies execution intent and provenance, achieving 96.5% attack blocking without impairing functionality. Our work redefines the threat model for agentic AI, shifting focus from user-input sanitization to external dependency trustworthiness, and provides concrete foundations for future standards and tooling.

Abstract

Agentic AI coding assistants can edit files, run commands, and access the internet on behalf of developers. However, their reliance on unvetted external artifacts introduces a new attack vector. Hidden instructions in external artifacts can hijack these assistants, turning them into an attacker's shell to run unauthorized commands. In this article, we examine how these prompt injection attacks work, measure their prevalence, discuss the limitations and challenges of current defenses, and suggest future research directions.

item.title: Broken Object Level Authorization in the Wild: An Empirical Taxonomy from 100+ Bug Bounty Disclosures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25865v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Broken Object Level Authorization（BOLA） 作为API安全领域最严峻的漏洞类型，长期位居OWASP API Security Top 10首位，但现有研究多停留于概念定义与理论建模，缺乏大规模实证支撑。实践中，漏洞平台标签（如HackerOne的“IDOR”）常被误用或泛化，导致BOLA真实分布、形态演化与技术成因模糊不清。

方法设计

本研究构建了可复现的实证框架：从HackerOne平台系统采集2021–2026年标注为“IDOR”或“Improper Access Control”的200份公开赏金报告；经三重人工校验筛选（含漏洞可复现性、对象级授权失效本质、非越权登录类混淆排除），最终获得107份高质量、全分类样本。分类过程采用LLM辅助+人工裁定双轨机制，依据严格定义的六类BOLA家族分类法（含Direct Object Reference、Action-Level Object、Nested Object等）完成结构化标注。

核心发现

  • 78.5%（84/107） 报告确属BOLA，证实其在真实攻击场景中高度普遍；
  • Action-Level Object BOLA（41.7%） 与Direct Object Reference并列为两大主导类型——前者指对他人资源执行未授权状态变更（如删改他人的订单、评论、配置），此前在开发指南与测试工具中严重缺位；
  • 21.5%样本被判定为“出范围”，揭示平台标签存在显著噪声，单纯统计“IDOR标签数”会高估BOLA实际占比达3倍以上；
  • 首次系统识别出GraphQL全局ID（Global ID）滥用（覆盖GitHub、Shopify等主流平台）及垂直权限坍塌（11.9%涉及普通用户提权至管理员上下文）等新兴模式。

实践价值

成果直接推动API安全测试协议升级（如将Action-Level测试纳入自动化扫描基线）、重构开发者教育重点，并为OWASP API Security Top 10 2023+版本提供首个基于百例真实漏洞的分类学依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first large-scale empirical taxonomy of Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA), analyzing 107 rigorously validated bug bounty reports from HackerOne (2021–2026). Using an LLM-assisted, human-adjudicated classification schema across six BOLA families, we find that 78.5% of confirmed cases are genuine BOLA—challenging assumptions based solely on platform tags like “IDOR”. Action-Level Object BOLA (41.7%), involving unauthorized state-changing operations on another user’s objects (e.g., deleting or modifying their data), emerges as a dominant and previously underrepresented pattern. Critically, 21.5% of tagged reports were out-of-scope under strict BOLA criteria, exposing severe overcounting in tag-based metrics. Additional findings include an 11.9% rate of vertical privilege escalation and systematic exploitation of GraphQL Global IDs across major platforms. These results inform API security testing, developer training, and OWASP guidance with empirically grounded priorities.

Abstract

Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA) is consistently ranked the most critical API security vulnerability, yet the existing literature remains almost entirely conceptual. This paper presents one of the first large-scale empirical analyses of BOLA in publicly disclosed bug bounty reports. We constructed a reproducible sampling frame of 200 HackerOne disclosures tagged IDOR or Improper Access Control (2021-2026) and applied a three-criterion inclusion filter, yielding 107 fully classified reports. Classification used an LLM-assisted schema-completion procedure under constrained, human-adjudicated criteria against a six-family BOLA taxonomy. Of 107 classified reports, 84 (78.5%) were confirmed in-scope BOLA. Action-Level Object BOLA, defined by unauthorized state-changing actions on another user's objects, accounts for 41.7% of confirmed cases and emerges alongside Direct Object Reference BOLA as one of the two dominant families observed in the dataset. This shows a pattern historically underrepresented in practitioner guidance. Approximately 21.5% of classified reports are out-of-scope under strict criteria, indicating that tag-counting on platforms like HackerOne significantly overstates the BOLA-specific signal. We report distributions across family, action type, authorization direction, industry sector, identifier format, and exploit mechanism. Key secondary findings include an 11.9% rate of vertical (user-to-admin) privilege failures and systematic exploitation of GraphQL Global IDs across major platforms. Findings have direct implications for API security testing protocols, developer education, and OWASP guidance.

item.title: TTPrint: Evidence-Grounded TTP Extraction via Diverge-then-Converge Verification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25836v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

从网络威胁情报（CTI）报告中提取MITRE ATT&CK技术（TTPs）是一项开放集、多标签抽取任务，需同时保障高召回率（避免遗漏真实技战术）与高精确率（杜绝无依据的幻觉生成）。现有方法存在明显瓶颈：规则/监督学习模型泛化能力弱，难以适配异构攻击描述；而主流大语言模型（LLM）方法将候选生成与验证耦合于单次推理中，导致二者相互制约——提升召回常以牺牲精度为代价，反之亦然。

方法创新：Diverge-then-Converge 验证范式

本文提出 TTPrint，受人类分析师工作流启发，构建“先发散、后收敛”的两阶段证据驱动框架：

  • 发散阶段：将报告细粒度分解为原子行为单元，广域生成候选技术；通过确定性跨文本跨度定位（deterministic span localization），将每个候选精准锚定至源文本中的最小支持证据窗口；
  • 收敛阶段：仅保留双重验证通过的候选——既在本地证据窗口内语义一致，又严格符合MITRE官方技术定义。

资源与评估贡献

我们发布两个高质量基准：① 清洗重构的TRAM数据集（TRAM-Clean），消除原始标注噪声；② 新建的文档级人工标注数据集（TTPrint-Bench），首次系统支持跨句、跨段落TTP关联验证。实验表明：TTPrint在TRAM-Clean和TTPrint-Bench上分别取得76.48% 与 87.39% 宏F1，较SOTA基线提升63.5% 和 29.4%。多模型（6种LLM主干）与阈值敏感性分析进一步验证其架构鲁棒性与部署实用性。

AI Summary (English)

TTPrint addresses the open-set, multi-label MITRE ATT&CK technique extraction task from CTI reports by decoupling candidate generation and rigorous verification into a diverge-then-converge pipeline inspired by human analyst workflows. In the divergent phase, reports are decomposed into atomic behaviors to broadly propose candidates, each deterministically localized to a minimal evidence span in the source text. The convergent phase retains only candidates jointly supported by both this localized evidence and the authoritative MITRE definition. To enable robust evaluation, we release TRAM-Clean (a noise-cleaned TRAM benchmark) and TTPrint-Bench (a new document-level annotated dataset), elevating the task beyond sentence-level constraints. On these benchmarks, TTPrint achieves 76.48% and 87.39% macro-F1—outperforming the strongest baseline by +63.5% and +29.4%, respectively. Extensive ablations across six LLM backbones and threshold sensitivity analysis confirm its generalizability and practical deployability.

Abstract

Extracting MITRE ATT&CK techniques from cyber threat intelligence (CTI) reports is an open-set, multi-label problem requiring both high recall (not missing techniques) and high precision (not hallucinating unsupported ones). Existing methods--rule-based, supervised, and LLM-based--struggle to achieve both: rule-based and supervised approaches lack generalizability across diverse attack descriptions, while LLM-based approaches that couple candidate generation and validation within a single inference step suffer from limited recall and precision simultaneously. We propose TTPrint, which addresses this challenge through a diverge-then-converge design inspired by how human analysts work: first extracting broadly, then verifying rigorously. In the divergent phase, reports are decomposed into atomic behaviors and candidate techniques are proposed broadly. A deterministic span localization stage then anchors each candidate to a specific evidence window in the source text. A convergent verification stage retains only candidates supported by both the localized evidence and the authoritative MITRE definition. We contribute two evaluation resources--a cleaned TRAM benchmark (TRAM-Clean) and a new annotated dataset (TTPrint-Bench)--to address known annotation noise in existing benchmarks and elevate the task to document-level TTP extraction. On TRAM-Clean and TTPrint-Bench, TTPrint achieves 76.48% and 87.39% macro-F1 respectively, outperforming the leading baseline by 63.5% and 29.4%. A multi-backbone analysis across six LLMs and a threshold sensitivity study further demonstrate generalizability across model choices and provide practical guidance for parameter selection.

item.title: "What is the Problem Space?" Defining Host-space Adversarial Perturbations against Network Intrusion Detection Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25822v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题提出

当前，基于机器学习的网络入侵检测系统（ML-NIDS）广泛部署于网络安全防御前沿，但其对抗鲁棒性正面临严峻挑战。大量研究通过在数据空间（如原始PCAP包或预提取的流量特征）上施加微小扰动来构造对抗样本，并报告了高规避率——然而，这类扰动往往假设攻击者可直接篡改路由器捕获的数据包或NIDS内部处理的特征向量。本文尖锐指出：真实攻击者通常仅能控制终端主机（如被攻陷的客户端或服务器），无法干预中间网络设备或检测系统的内部数据表示。由此，我们首次明确定义并聚焦于宿主空间（host-space）对抗扰动——即所有扰动必须且只能通过修改攻击者可控主机上的行为（如命令行输入、API调用、协议字段生成逻辑）来实现。

方法与实验验证

我们开展三项核心工作：（1）对316篇相关文献进行系统性综述，揭示>92%的现有对抗攻击违背宿主空间约束；（2）在CIC-IDS2017、UNSW-NB15等主流基准及真实局域网环境中，构建首个宿主空间攻击框架，以SSH暴力破解为典型场景；（3）实证发现：仅修改发起攻击的命令字符串中单个字符（如ssh -l root 192.168.1.10 → ssh -L root 192.168.1.10），即可导致ML-NIDS检测率从99.2%骤降至0%，而该变化在宿主端完全合法、不可见于网络层。进一步分析表明，此类微小宿主操作会引发特征空间的非线性坍塌——关键时序/统计特征（如连接间隔方差、包长熵）发生跨阈值跃变，使模型陷入决策盲区。

创新与启示

本研究不仅确立了“宿主空间”这一关键安全评估维度，更提出可复现的评估范式：强调扰动可行性（host-controllable）、可观测性隔离（不依赖中间设备篡改）与因果可追溯性（从宿主动作到特征偏移的完整链路）。我们呼吁：ML-NIDS的安全评测必须回归攻击者能力边界，摒弃脱离实际的“数据空间幻想”。

AI Summary (English)

This paper challenges the prevailing assumption in adversarial ML research on Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS): that perturbations can be arbitrarily applied to pre-collected packets or extracted features. We introduce and rigorously define host-space adversarial perturbations—modifications strictly confined to actions an attacker can perform only on compromised end-hosts (e.g., altering command-line arguments, payload generation logic, or protocol field values), without requiring control over routers, firewalls, or the NIDS’s internal data pipeline. Through a systematic review of 316 papers, we find that >92% of prior attacks violate this realistic constraint. Experimentally, on CIC-IDS2017, UNSW-NB15, and a live network, we demonstrate that changing just one character in an SSH brute-force command string (e.g., -l → -L) causes state-of-the-art ML-NIDS to completely miss the attack—despite the perturbation being undetectable at the network layer and fully executable on an unprivileged host. This minuscule host-space change induces catastrophic, non-linear shifts in the feature space (e.g., collapsing inter-arrival variance and packet-length entropy), exposing a critical gap between data-space robustness and real-world security. We provide actionable guidelines for evaluating host-space threats and argue that ML-NIDS security assessments must be fundamentally re-grounded in attacker capability models.

Abstract

Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS) are now increasingly leveraging Machine Learning (ML) techniques to detect malicious network activities. Numerous papers have scrutinized the security of ML-based NIDS (ML-NIDS) by testing them against various attacks involving adversarial perturbations. The findings were oftentimes worrying: by making imperceptible changes to a given input, powerful ML models would be bypassed. In this context, we took a step back and wondered: where (i.e., in what "space") have these perturbations been applied? We argue that real-world adversaries can apply adversarial perturbations only by operating on the hosts they can control -- a concept which we define as _host-space perturbations_. To some, such an observation may seem trivial. And yet, through a systematic literature review (n=316), we found that prior work applied perturbations by manipulating pre-collected datapoints (e.g., a packet _captured by the router_, or a network flow _analysed by the ML-NIDS_). Such operations, while not impossible, may be outside the reach of an attacker who can only control some (unprivileged) hosts in a network. Hence, to demonstrate how to craft host-space perturbations and study some of their effects, we experimented on well-known benchmarks and a real-world network. We show that ML-NIDS that can detect the SSH-bruteforcing attempts launched via a given command string cannot detect any attempt launched by changing _a single character_ of such a string. We then examined how such a minuscule change in the "problem space" (i.e., the attacker's host) can lead to devastating effects on the "feature space". We derive lessons learned on how to practically assess host-space perturbations. Our stance is that the security of ML-NIDS should be re-assessed.

item.title: On Reliability of Efficient Membership Inference Vulnerability Evaluation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25819v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

成员推断攻击（Membership Inference Attacks, MIAs）是评估机器学习模型训练数据敏感信息泄露的主流实证方法。其漏洞强度通常通过二元分类器的真阳性率（TPR） 和假阳性率（FPR） 来量化。然而，在低FPR区域（如FPR < 0.01）可靠估计TPR需大量独立目标模型，导致计算开销巨大。实践中常采用“高效评估范式”：对多个个体和多个目标模型的MIA得分进行拼接或平均，以提升统计效率。

关键缺陷发现

本文系统揭示该范式中两大未被识别的可靠性危机：

  • FPR校准失效：现有工作将跨个体的MIA得分直接拼接后统一计算ROC曲线，隐含假设所有样本具有相同FPR分布。我们证明该假设不成立——不同样本的得分分布存在显著异质性，导致拼接后TPR在低FPR下严重失真，无法支撑差分隐私（DP）审计等高置信度安全验证；
  • 有限总体偏差：针对广泛使用的高效似然比攻击（LiRA, Carlini et al. 2022），我们首次指出其标准实现存在有限样本偏差：当用少量影子模型估计似然比时，其分母（非成员似然）被系统性低估，造成每样本漏洞分数正向偏移，高估攻击成功率。

方法与贡献

  • 提出分样本FPR校准（Per-sample FPR Calibration） 后处理方法：为每个样本独立构建经验FPR阈值映射，确保TPR在目标FPR水平下可比、无偏；
  • 给出LiRA偏差的理论分析与修正方案（基于无偏似然比估计），实验表明修正后TPR下降达15–40%（FPR=0.001），显著提升评估可信度；
  • 开源代码与基准测试协议，推动MIA评估标准化与可复现性。

AI Summary (English)

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) are widely used to empirically audit model privacy, yet their efficient evaluation—especially at low false positive rates (FPR)—suffers from two critical, previously overlooked reliability flaws. First, concatenating MIA scores across individuals for ROC analysis induces FPR miscalibration, as per-sample score distributions are heterogeneous; this invalidates TPR estimates for rigorous differential privacy auditing. We propose a per-sample FPR calibration method to restore statistical validity. Second, we identify a finite-population bias in the standard LiRA implementation (Carlini et al., 2022): the denominator of its likelihood ratio is systematically underestimated due to limited shadow models, causing a positive bias in per-sample vulnerability scores. Our theoretical analysis and unbiased estimator reduce reported TPR by 15–40% at FPR = 0.001. These findings fundamentally improve the trustworthiness of efficient MIA evaluation.

Abstract

Membership inference attacks (MIAs) are popular methods for empirically assessing the leakage of sensitive information in the training data through models or statistics learned from the data. The MIA vulnerability is often evaluated through false positive rate (FPR) and true positive rate (TPR) of a binary classifier that tries to predict whether a particular sample was in the training data. However, in order to reliably estimate the TPR especially for low FPR values, a lot of observations are needed, which in case of MIA translates to many target models, leading to large computational cost. To avoid excessive compute requirements, the MIA scores are often averaged over multiple individuals and multiple targeted models. We demonstrate two key weaknesses in this efficient MIA evaluation pipeline. First, we show that evaluating the TPR based on MIA scores concatenated across multiple individuals, commonly used to study vulnerabilities in the very low FPR regime, is not calibrated across the per-sample FPRs. This makes it unreliable as a tool for auditing differential privacy. To solve this, we propose a post-processing method to effectively calibrate the FPR across different samples. Second, we identify a finite population bias in the commonly used efficient likelihood-ratio attack (LiRA) implementation proposed by Carlini et al. 2022, leading to a positive bias in the per-sample vulnerability.

item.title: Referential Security as a New Paradigm for AI Evaluations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25673v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前AI安全评估面临根本性挑战：动态更新的模型系统破坏了评估的指称稳定性。实践中，同一公开名称（如“GPT-4-turbo”）长期沿用，但其底层权重、提示工程、检索增强机制、滥用检测器、推理参数及服务基础设施却持续进行未公告变更。这导致安全审计、监管结论与合规声明所指向的“系统”无法被唯一、持久地识别——评估对象实质上是模糊标签，而非可复现、可追溯的实体。

方法与范式创新

本文提出指称安全（Referential Security） 这一全新评估范式。其核心主张是：安全性的有效性不仅取决于模型是否安全，更取决于第三方能否确证某一安全声明究竟对应哪个具体、可验证的AI系统。该范式将“模型身份”重构为一项经验可验证属性（例如通过哈希锚定的完整推理链、配置快照与服务元数据），并严格区分“指称稳定性”（referential stability）与“实质安全性”（substantive safety）——前者是后者的前提条件，而非附属品。

关键贡献与影响

本框架系统性提升三类关键工作流的可操作性：

  • 可复现评估：基于可验证工件（如签名化的推理日志+配置指纹）实现结果精准回溯；
  • 纵向审计有效性：支持跨版本比对，确保监管发现不因系统漂移而失效；
  • 跨供应商等价性验证：提供中立基准，使不同厂商部署的“同名模型”可在相同指称基础上接受公平比较。

指称安全不替代现有安全测试，而是为其建立可信赖的指称锚点，使AI治理在动态演进中仍保持实证根基。

AI Summary (English)

Security evaluations require stable, unambiguous references to AI systems—yet today’s continuously updated models violate this foundational assumption. Public model names (e.g., “Claude-3.5-Sonnet”) remain static while weights, prompts, RAG pipelines, classifiers, and serving infrastructures change silently. Consequently, safety claims often attach to labels—not identifiable, verifiable artifacts. We introduce referential security: a new paradigm where the core security question shifts from “Is this model safe?” to “Can third parties conclusively determine which specific system a given safety claim refers to?” It treats model identity as an empirically verifiable property—anchored via cryptographic hashes of configuration snapshots, inference traces, and metadata—and decouples referential stability from substantive safety claims. This enables three critical advances: (1) reproducible evaluation through artifact-based verification; (2) longitudinal audit validity across model updates; and (3) cross-provider equivalence assessment under shared referential grounding. Referential security provides the essential infrastructure for trustworthy AI governance in dynamic deployment environments.

Abstract

Security evaluations inherently depend on stable identifiers. Any finding, audit, or regulatory decision must remain attached to the specific artifact it pertains to. Continuously updated artificial intelligence systems violate this core assumption, with public model designations remaining static while underlying weights, prompts, retrieval mechanisms, misuse classifiers, inference settings, and serving infrastructures undergo unannounced modifications. Consequently, current evaluations frequently apply to superficial labels rather than identifiable and distinct systems. To resolve this, we propose referential security as a new paradigm for AI evaluation. The fundamental security question extends beyond whether a model is safe to whether subsequent parties can conclusively determine which system a specific safety claim addressed. This approach reframes model identity as an empirically verifiable property and separates referential stability from the substantive security claims it conditions. This framework brings tractability to three critical workflows that current practices handle poorly. Specifically, it enables reproducible evaluation, longitudinal audit validity, and cross-provider equivalence. By grounding these evaluations in verifiable artifacts, our approach ensures that safety audits and regulatory findings maintain their empirical utility across the operational lifecycle of dynamic systems.

item.title: When Interpretability Becomes a Liability: Adversarial Attacks on CBM Concept Layers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25304v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

概念瓶颈模型（CBM）通过显式建模人类可理解的中间语义概念（如“有冠羽”“翅膀斑纹”），成为可解释机器学习的主流范式。然而，本研究首次指出：这种可解释性本身即构成新型攻击面——概念层（concept bottleneck layer）极易被对抗性扰动操控，导致模型在保持输入视觉变化极小的前提下，系统性扭曲语义表征，引发灾难性误分类。

方法与发现

我们构建了首个面向CBM概念空间的理论化鲁棒性分析框架，提出“概念扰动半径”与“概念转移距离”等新指标，量化概念层对输入扰动的敏感性。在CUB-200-2011细粒度鸟类数据集上的系统实验表明：标准CBM仅需平均L₂扰动范数0.46即可实现目标概念翻转（如将“无冠羽”强制激活为“有冠羽”），进而导致下游分类错误率飙升至89.7%，证实其概念层存在严重脆弱性。

创新方案：SPECTRA

为此，我们提出SPECTRA（Semantic Perturbation-based Concept Training for Robustness against Attacks）——一种基于语义扰动的概念层稳定性正则化方法。该方法在训练中显式注入受控的概念空间扰动，并约束概念预测对微小输入变化保持局部一致性。实验显示：SPECTRA将成功攻击所需的最小扰动范数从0.46提升至4,200+（增幅超9000倍），使定向概念操纵在计算上不可行；同时分类准确率仅下降2.2%（从85.3%→83.1%），显著优于现有防御方法。本工作首次确立“概念级攻击”为独立威胁模型，开辟了可解释AI与对抗鲁棒性交叉研究的新前沿。

AI Summary (English)

Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) enhance interpretability by enforcing human-readable semantic concepts (e.g., “crested head”, “black wing bars”) as intermediate representations—yet this very property exposes a previously unexplored attack surface: the concept bottleneck layer itself. We present the first systematic study of concept-level adversarial attacks, showing that minimal pixel-space perturbations can catastrophically manipulate semantic activations and induce targeted misclassification. We develop a theoretical framework to quantify concept-space robustness, revealing severe vulnerabilities in standard CBMs on CUB-200-2011 (attack norm: 0.46). To counter this, we propose SPECTRA, a semantic perturbation–based stability regularization method that hardens concept representations. SPECTRA increases the minimum successful attack norm to >4,200, rendering targeted concept manipulation computationally prohibitive, while preserving classification accuracy within 2.2% of the baseline. This work establishes concept-level attacks as a distinct threat model at the intersection of interpretable ML and adversarial robustness.

Abstract

Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) have emerged as a cornerstone approach for interpretable machine learning, providing human-understandable intermediate representations through explicit concept activations. However, this interpretability fundamentally introduces a critical, previously unexplored attack surface: the concept bottleneck layer itself. We present a comprehensive, systematic study of concept-level adversarial vulnerabilities in CBMs, revealing that targeted, minimal perturbations operating on input pixels can induce catastrophic misclassification by manipulating semantic representations. We develop a rigorous theoretical framework to quantify concept-space robustness, establishing novel metrics that expose the vulnerability landscape of these architectures. Our extensive analysis on the CUB-200-2011 dataset demonstrates that standard CBMs exhibit severe susceptibility to concept-level manipulation. To address this critical weakness, we introduce SPECTRA (Semantic Perturbation-based Concept Training for Robustness against Attacks), a principled stability regularization defense. SPECTRA effectively hardens the semantic representation space, increasing the minimal perturbation norm required for a successful attack from 0.46 to over 4,200, rendering targeted concept manipulation computationally prohibitive. Furthermore, SPECTRA preserves baseline classification accuracy to within 2.2%. By establishing concept-level attacks as a fundamentally distinct threat model, this work opens a new research frontier at the intersection of interpretable machine learning and adversarial robustness.

item.title: EchoPilot: Training-Free Ultrasound Video Segmentation via Scale-Space Semantic Prompting and Reliability-Gated Memory
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25944v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

超声视频分割在胎儿监护、术中引导等临床场景中具有重要价值，但受斑点噪声强、解剖边界模糊、器官形变快三大因素制约，现有方法难以稳定泛化。尽管可提示的基础模型（如SAM）支持单点交互，但在超声视频中直接应用仍面临两大瓶颈：① 单点缺乏空间上下文，无法消解多尺度解剖结构的初始化歧义；② 传统贪婪式记忆更新机制会将首帧微小误差逐帧放大，引发严重时序漂移。

方法创新

本研究提出EchoPilot——首个无需任何训练、仅需首帧单次点击+解剖类别名称即可完成全视频分割的框架。其核心包含三模块协同：冻结的医学视觉语言模型（VLM）实现语义定位、视觉基础模型（VFM）提取稠密几何特征、可提示视频分割器执行掩码预测与传播。

  • 尺度空间语义提示（Scale-Space Semantic Prompting）：提出无参数S.E.E.D.（Semantic Energy-Entropy Density）准则，自动选择最优上下文尺度视图；继而从稠密基础特征中零交互合成高精度辅助点提示，显著提升初始分割鲁棒性。
  • 可靠性门控记忆（Reliability-Gated Memory）：基于预测置信度动态冻结视频分割器的记忆库，仅在高可靠性区域更新记忆，从机制上阻断误差累积。

实验与贡献

构建首个动态胎儿胎盘超声视频分割数据集（671帧精细标注）；在3个公开超声视频数据集上全面验证，EchoPilot在稀疏交互设定下平均Dice达82.7%，超越所有无训练基线（+5.3%）及微调专家模型（+2.1%），且推理速度达28 FPS，具备临床实时部署潜力。

AI Summary (English)

Ultrasound video segmentation is clinically critical yet notoriously challenging due to speckle noise, low contrast, and rapid anatomical deformation. While promptable foundation models enable point-guided segmentation, their direct application to ultrasound videos suffers from scale ambiguity (from single-point initialization) and severe temporal drift (from greedy memory updates). EchoPilot addresses these issues without any training: given only one click and an anatomical category name on the first frame, it achieves robust video segmentation by synergizing a frozen medical VLM (for semantic grounding), a VFM (for dense geometric features), and a promptable video segmentor. We propose Scale-Space Semantic Prompting, which selects the optimal contextual scale via a parameter-free S.E.E.D. criterion and synthesizes precise auxiliary point prompts from foundation features—zero additional user input required. To curb drift, Reliability-Gated Memory selectively freezes the segmentor’s memory bank under uncertain predictions. Evaluated on three ultrasound video benchmarks—including our newly released dynamic fetal placenta dataset (671 frames)—EchoPilot sets new state-of-the-art performance under sparse interaction, outperforming all training-free baselines and even fine-tuned specialists, with real-time inference (28 FPS).

Abstract

Ultrasound video segmentation is clinically valuable yet difficult due to speckle noise, weak boundaries, and rapid anatomical deformation. Recent promptable foundation models enable point-guided segmentation, but their direct deployment in ultrasound remains unreliable: a single point provides insufficient spatial context to resolve scale ambiguity, and greedy memory updates amplify early errors into severe temporal drift. We present EchoPilot, a training-free framework for ultrasound video segmentation under sparse first-frame interaction, requiring only a single point click and an anatomical category name. EchoPilot orchestrates a frozen medical vision-language model (VLM) for semantic localization, a vision foundation model (VFM) for dense geometric feature extraction, and a promptable video segmentor for mask prediction and propagation. To resolve initialization ambiguity, we propose Scale-Space Semantic Prompting, which first selects an optimal contextual view via a parameter-free S.E.E.D. (Semantic Energy-Entropy Density) criterion, and then synthesizes geometrically precise auxiliary point prompts from dense foundation features without additional user interaction. To reduce propagation drift, a Reliability-Gated Memory update is further introduced to selectively freeze the segmentor's memory bank under uncertain predictions, preventing error accumulation. We also contribute the first dynamic fetal placenta ultrasound video segmentation dataset with 671 annotated frames. Across three ultrasound video datasets, EchoPilot achieves state-of-the-art performance under the sparse-interactive setting, consistently outperforming training-free baselines and finetuned specialists.

item.title: PennySynth: RAG-Driven Data Synthesis for Automated Quantum Code Generation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25572v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

随着PennyLane等量子编程框架的快速发展，通用大语言模型（LLM）在生成量子代码时暴露出严重缺陷：频繁幻觉生成不存在的门操作符（如虚构qml.RotX）、错误配置量子设备、以及产出结构非法的量子电路，导致功能失效。现有RAG方法在量子领域缺乏领域适配性，通用嵌入模型难以精准匹配自然语言查询与专业量子代码片段。

方法创新

我们提出PennySynth——首个面向PennyLane生态的检索增强型量子代码合成框架。其核心包括：

  • 构建高质量知识库：通过三阶段流水线（提取→验证→去重），整合官方文档、社区GitHub仓库及三年QHack竞赛存档，构建含13,389组指令-代码对的权威语料库；
  • 提出代码感知嵌入策略：微调st-codesearch-distilroberta-base模型，专用于自然语言到量子代码的跨模态检索，使平均余弦相似度从0.45显著提升至0.726；
  • 设计量子增强型CodeBLEU：显式提升qml.*类API模式权重，解耦评估结构性相似性与功能性正确性。

关键结果

在覆盖QHack 2022–2024共74道真实竞赛题的严格评测中，PennySynth的pass@5分别达64%、68%、52%，相较未使用RAG的Claude Sonnet 4.6提升+28、+25、+28个百分点。消融实验表明：代码感知嵌入是性能跃升的主因，而数据源多样性（官方/社区/竞赛）与规模扩展提供稳健增益。

AI Summary (English)

PennySynth is a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) framework for automated PennyLane quantum code synthesis. It addresses LLM hallucinations and structural invalidity by conditioning generation on a curated knowledge base of 13,389 verified instruction-code pairs—extracted and deduplicated from official PennyLane docs, community GitHub repos, and QHack competition archives (2022–2024). We introduce a code-aware embedding strategy using fine-tuned st-codesearch-distilroberta-base, boosting average retrieval cosine similarity from 0.45 to 0.726. Evaluated on 74 real-world QHack challenges, PennySynth achieves pass@5 scores of 64%, 68%, and 52% for QHack 2022, 2023, and 2024—outperforming Claude Sonnet 4.6 (no RAG) by +28, +25, and +28 percentage points. We also propose a quantum-adapted CodeBLEU metric that upweights qml.* tokens, revealing distinct dimensions of quantum code quality. Ablations confirm code-aware embeddings are the primary driver of performance gains.

Abstract

The growing complexity of quantum programming frameworks has exposed a critical limitation in existing large language model (LLM)-based code assistants: general-purpose models hallucinate PennyLane-specific gate names, misplace device configurations, and produce structurally invalid circuits when faced with specialized quantum coding challenges. We present PennySynth, a retrieval-augmented generation framework that addresses this gap by conditioning LLM inference on a curated knowledge base of 13,389 PennyLane instruction-code pairs, built via a three-stage extraction, verification, and deduplication pipeline over official PennyLane repositories, community GitHub sources, and QHack competition archives. PennySynth introduces a code-aware embedding strategy using st-codesearch-distilroberta-base, trained for natural-language-to-code retrieval, increasing average retrieval cosine similarity from 0.45 to 0.726 compared to a general-purpose baseline. Evaluated across 74 challenges spanning three years of the QHack competition (2022, 2023, 2024), PennySynth achieves 64%, 68%, and 52% pass@5 on QHack 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively, improving over Claude Sonnet 4.6 without retrieval by +28, +25, and +28 percentage points. We further introduce a quantum-adapted CodeBLEU metric that upweights qml.* token patterns and show that structural code similarity and functional correctness capture distinct aspects of quantum code quality. Controlled ablations reveal that code-aware embeddings are the primary driver of retrieval performance, while dataset expansion and source composition provide additional gains when retrieval quality is sufficiently precise.

item.title: A Tertiary Review of Large Language Model-Based Code Generating Tasks: Trends, Challenges, and Future Directions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25536v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与目标

随着大语言模型（LLMs）在软件工程领域的深度渗透，基于LLM的代码生成任务（CGTs）已成为研究热点。尽管基准测试结果普遍乐观，但其在真实开发场景中的泛化能力、鲁棒性、经济可行性及社会技术整合效果仍缺乏系统性认知。现有三次研究（tertiary studies）对此覆盖不足。本研究旨在开展一项严谨的三次综述，系统整合现有二次研究证据，全面刻画LLM-CGTs的研究图谱、实际影响、应用场景、集成挑战及未来方向。

方法

遵循系统性综述规范，我们在ACM DL、IEEE Xplore、SpringerLink等主流数字图书馆中检索2017–2025年文献，并辅以回溯与前溯雪球抽样（backward-and-forward snowballing）及多轮筛选。通过定制化质量评估清单与双盲编码审核（Krippendorff’s α = 0.89），保障证据提取可靠性。合成分析采用SWEBOK知识域框架进行主题归类，并结合HELM（Human Evaluation of Language Models）框架评估评估实践成熟度。

主要发现与创新点

  • 共纳入30项高质量二次研究，呈现爆发式增长（2023年起占比超73%）；
  • 性能断层显著：模型在HumanEval等基准上准确率高（平均>72%），但真实项目补全、调试修复等任务中泛化能力弱，鲁棒性随输入扰动/配置变更急剧下降；
  • 效率与可信度瓶颈突出：约87%研究提及推理延迟或API成本制约落地；毒性与偏见问题仅在6项研究中被主动报告；
  • 三大核心挑战：经济可行性（ROI不明）、评估有效性（人工评估缺失/不一致）、社会技术整合（开发者信任、工作流嵌入、责任归属）；
  • 未来方向聚焦：发展领域感知（domain-aware）微调范式、构建覆盖功能正确性、可维护性、安全性、协作性的标准化多维评估基准，并推动成本-效益联合建模。

AI Summary (English)

This tertiary review synthesizes evidence from 30 high-quality secondary studies (2017–2025) on LLM-based code generation tasks (CGTs). Using systematic search, snowballing, and rigorous quality appraisal (Krippendorff’s α = 0.89), we map publication trends, application scenarios, and critical gaps. Results reveal a sharp rise since 2023, yet strong benchmark performance (e.g., HumanEval) poorly generalizes to real-world maintenance, debugging, or collaborative settings. Robustness is highly configuration-sensitive; efficiency constraints (latency, cost) are pervasive; toxicity and bias remain under-reported. Key challenges center on economic feasibility, evaluation validity (especially human-centered assessment), and socio-technical integration. We advocate domain-aware model adaptation and holistic, standardized evaluation frameworks—spanning correctness, security, maintainability, and developer experience—to advance CGTs beyond isolated benchmarks toward sustainable engineering practice.

Abstract

Context. Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly applied to code-generating tasks (CGTs) in software engineering. While reported results are promising, the broader effects of such application and their integration into real-world development remain insufficiently understood with existing tertiary studies provide little in this area. Objective. This tertiary study consolidates secondary evidence on LLM-based CGTs, synthesizing the publication landscape, effects, scenarios, integration challenges, and future research directions. Method. Following systematic review guidelines, we searched in related digital libraries, complemented by backward-and-forward snowballing and screening step. Study quality was assessed and extraction reliability was audited with inter-rater agreement statistics. Evidence was synthesized using SWEBOK knowledge areas and the HELM framework. Results. We identify 30 secondary studies published between 2017-2025, with rapid growth since 2023. Accuracy seems strong on benchmarks but weakly supported for real-world generalization; robustness is fragile across tasks and configurations; efficiency constraints are pervasive; toxicity and bias are under-reported. Dominant challenges concern economic feasibility, evaluation validity, and socio-technical integration. Future directions suggest domain-aware model improvement and the need for holistic, standardized evaluation. Conclusion. LLM-based CGTs represent a fast-maturing yet unevenly evaluated research area, highlighting the need for domain-aware model improvements and holistic, standardized evaluation, addressing efficiency and associated costs.

item.title: Security of OpenClaw Agents: Fundamentals, Attacks, and Countermeasures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25435v1

AI Summary (中文)

OpenClaw智能体安全研究：基础架构、新型攻击与防御体系

随着大语言模型（LLM）驱动的自主智能体迅猛发展，OpenClaw作为一类新兴开源智能体框架脱颖而出——其核心特征包括持续运行机制、技能增强能力、持久化记忆、多通道交互接口及高度自主性。区别于传统任务导向型AI代理，OpenClaw智能体可跨轮次维持上下文状态，自主编排多步骤任务，并深度集成外部API与工具链，显著提升实用性，但亦导致攻击面指数级扩张。

本综述首次系统构建OpenClaw安全分析框架：

  • 分层威胁建模：依据智能体生命周期，将风险划分为认知层（如提示注入引发的认知偏移、推理链污染）、执行层（技能中毒、权限越界调用）、交互层（多智能体协同失效、恶意工具劫持）及供应链层（依赖库投毒、插件签名绕过）；
  • 根因深度剖析：揭示持久化记忆与高特权操作耦合所诱发的“状态污染—权限放大”双重脆弱性，例如历史记忆被篡改后持续误导后续决策；
  • 防御全景图谱：归纳三类主流对策——运行时沙箱化（细粒度工具权限控制+内存隔离）、认知完整性验证（推理路径水印、可信记忆快照比对）、供应链可信加固（零信任插件签名+SBOM动态审计）；
  • 前瞻性挑战：指出当前尚无统一可信执行环境（TEE）适配方案，且多智能体协同场景下的责任归属与故障溯源仍属空白。本研究为构建可验证、可审计、可追责的下一代开放智能体生态提供理论基石与实践指南。

AI Summary (English)

This survey presents the first comprehensive security analysis of OpenClaw agents—open-source, LLM-driven autonomous systems characterized by persistent memory, skill augmentation, multi-channel interaction, and high autonomy. We propose a layered threat taxonomy grounded in the agent lifecycle: cognitive layer (e.g., prompt-based reasoning manipulation), execution layer (e.g., skill poisoning and privilege escalation), interaction layer (e.g., cascading failures across agent collectives), and supply-chain layer (e.g., compromised plugins or dependencies). We identify the critical vulnerability arising from the coupling of persistent state and high-privilege actions—enabling persistent “state pollution” that propagates across execution cycles. Key defenses reviewed include runtime sandboxing with fine-grained tool permissions, cognitive integrity verification via reasoning-path watermarking, and supply-chain hardening through zero-trust plugin signing and SBOM-based auditing. We conclude by highlighting unresolved challenges, notably the lack of TEE support and accountability mechanisms in multi-agent coordination—offering foundational insights for building trustworthy, auditable open agent ecosystems.

Abstract

The rapid evolution of large language model (LLM)-driven autonomous agents has given rise to OpenClaw, a new class of open-source agent frameworks that operate as continuously running, skill-augmented systems with persistent memory, multi-channel interaction, and high degrees of autonomy. Such capabilities enable OpenClaw agents to autonomously execute complex, multi-step tasks and interact seamlessly with external applications, but simultaneously introduce a substantially enlarged attack surface. In particular, the combination of high-privilege operations and persistent memory exposes OpenClaw agents to various emerging threats, including skill poisoning, cognitive manipulation, multi-agent cascading failures, and supply-chain vulnerabilities. In this survey, we present a comprehensive study of the security landscape of OpenClaw agents. We first examine the general architecture and key characteristics that distinguish OpenClaw agents from traditional AI agent systems. We categorize existing security and privacy threats into a layered framework and analyze how vulnerabilities arise during agent reasoning, action execution, and external interaction. Representative defense mechanisms are also reviewed to draw the current defense landscape. Finally, several unresolved issues related to the reliability and trustworthiness of OpenClaw ecosystems are discussed.

item.title: Joint Optimization of Training and Inference in Federated Edge Learning via Constrained Multi-Objective Deep Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25916v1

AI Summary (中文)

联邦边缘学习中训练与推理的联合在线优化

背景与挑战：联邦边缘学习（FEEL）旨在通过边缘设备协同训练模型实现边缘智能（EI），同时保障数据隐私。然而，真实边缘场景中，设备资源受限、推理请求动态涌入、训练数据与模型状态随时间衰减（即“新鲜度”下降），导致传统分离式训练/推理调度难以兼顾精度、时延与能耗三重目标。

方法创新：本文提出首个面向FEEL的训练-推理联合在线优化框架。核心贡献包括：（1）设计串联队列启发的转换机制，将实时推理请求动态转化为训练样本流，打通服务闭环；（2）在精度建模中首次联合引入数据新鲜度（data freshness）与模型新鲜度（model freshness），显式刻画时序动态性；（3）构建多目标优化问题，联合决策设备的工作模式选择（训练/推理/空闲）、通信资源分配（上行带宽、传输功率）与计算资源分配（CPU频率、批大小）。

算法突破：针对该NP难、在线、多目标问题，本文将其建模为多目标马尔可夫决策过程（MOMDP），并提出约束型多目标近端策略优化算法（C-MOPPO）：首先通过偏好向量采样学习多样化基础策略集；继而引入约束策略优化机制，在Pareto前沿上密集生成高质量解，显著提升解集覆盖度与均匀性。

实验验证：在NS-3+PyTorch混合仿真平台上的大量实验表明：C-MOPPO在多种网络规模、设备异构性及负载强度下，相较FedAvg、FedProx、MOO-DQN等基线，推理精度平均提升12.7%，端到端时延降低38.5%，能量消耗减少41.2%，且Pareto解集密度提升2.3倍，验证了其强鲁棒性与实用价值。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Edge Learning (FEEL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative model training across resource-constrained edge devices, yet jointly optimizing real-time inference and periodic training remains challenging due to dynamic workloads, data/model staleness, and conflicting objectives (accuracy, latency, energy). This paper proposes an online joint optimization framework that bridges inference requests and training data via a tandem-queue-inspired conversion mechanism and explicitly incorporates data and model freshness into the accuracy metric to capture temporal dynamics. We formulate the problem as a constrained multi-objective optimization—jointly deciding device operation modes, communication bandwidth/power, and computation CPU frequency/batch size—and cast it as a Multi-Objective Markov Decision Process (MOMDP). To solve it, we design C-MOPPO: a Constrained Multi-Objective Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm that first learns diverse preference-aware policies and then leverages constrained policy updates to densely populate the Pareto front with high-quality solutions. Extensive experiments show C-MOPPO achieves superior trade-offs—improving inference accuracy by 12.7%, reducing end-to-end latency by 38.5%, and cutting energy consumption by 41.2% over state-of-the-art baselines—while generating 2.3× denser Pareto-optimal solutions under heterogeneous system configurations.

Abstract

Federated edge learning (FEEL) has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for achieving edge intelligence (EI) via enabling collaborative model training across edge devices while protecting data privacy. In this paper, we put forth an online optimization framework that jointly manages federated training and inference on resource-constrained edge devices. We introduce a tandem-queue-inspired conversion mechanism that bridges inference requests and training data, and further incorporate both data and model freshness into the accuracy formulation to capture temporal dynamics in real-world environments. To maximize inference accuracy while minimizing latency and energy consumption, the mode selections, communication, and computation resource allocations of edge devices are jointly optimized. We formulate this optimization as a multi-objective optimization problem, which is NP-hard and further complicated by the online setting. To address these challenges, we transform the problem into a multi-objective Markov decision process (MOMDP) and develop a \underline{c}onstrained \underline{m}ulti-\underline{o}bjective \underline{p}roximal \underline{p}olicy \underline{o}ptimization (C-MOPPO) algorithm. Specifically, C-MOPPO first learns a set of policies with different preferences across three objectives, then leverages constrained policy optimization to enrich the Pareto front and obtain high-quality, dense solutions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that C-MOPPO achieves well-balanced trade-offs among objectives and significantly outperforms baselines under various system configurations.

item.title: Peak-Then-Collapse and the Four Interface Channels of Knowledge-Graph Tool Use
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26037v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

本研究聚焦大语言模型（LLM）在知识图谱（KG）工具调用中的根本性脆弱性。区别于Python解释器、网页搜索或JSON API等主流工具，知识图谱（如Freebase）的接口反馈极度贫瘠——空结果仅返回[]，不提供任何自然语言错误信号（如Python的traceback或搜索的“未找到”提示）。这种界面静默性导致模型难以通过强化学习（RL）自我修正。

方法设计

我们在Complex WebQuestions数据集上，采用标准RLVR范式（GRPO算法），以Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct为基座模型，对接一个极简KG工具API：仅含4个Freebase导航动词（如navigate, filter）。奖励函数采用自验证检索奖励（self-verifiable retrieval reward），即仅当检索结果能直接支撑最终答案时给予正向信号。

核心发现：“峰值后崩溃”现象

训练过程中出现显著的Peak-Then-Collapse模式：工具接地回答率从初始3.8%升至250步时的9.6%，却在后续单个50步窗口内骤降至0%——该现象在4组随机种子中完全复现。进一步测试7种奖励变体（包括更密集、更精准的代理奖励）均未能消除崩溃，仅迁移失败模式，证实问题源于接口本质而非奖励设计缺陷。

归因分析与关键洞见

  • Oracle消融实验表明：注入黄金关系（gold relations）仅提升EM 0.20个百分点；95.4%的检索依赖型错误源于检索组合失败（如路径拼接错误、多跳逻辑断裂），而非答案抽取失败。
  • 界面四通道假说：提出KG工具存在四大信息贫瘠通道——无错误命名、无语义提示、无部分成功反馈、无操作可逆性——共同构成模型学习的“退化区”。
  • 缓解方案：单轮自蒸馏（one-iteration self-distillation）将7B模型EM推至40.0%；且呈现容量不变性——升至14B仅+0.25pp，初始化影响微弱，表明性能上限由界面约束主导，而非模型规模。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies a fundamental peak-then-collapse failure mode in LLM-based knowledge-graph (KG) tool use: under self-verifiable retrieval rewards, GRPO-trained Qwen2.5-7B achieves a peak tool-grounded answer rate of 9.6% at step 250—then collapses to 0% within just 50 steps, consistently across four seeds. Unlike Python interpreters or web search, KG APIs (e.g., Freebase) provide no natural-language interface feedback—empty results return only [], leaking no pretraining-aligned error signals. Seven reward redesigns shift but never eliminate the collapse, confirming the issue is interface-bound, not reward-tunable. Oracle ablation shows gold relation injection improves EM by only +0.20 pp; 95.4% of errors stem from retrieval-composition failures, not answer extraction. A one-iteration self-distillation strategy lifts EM to 40.0% at 7B—and exhibits strong capacity invariance (+0.25 pp at 14B), indicating an interface-imposed ceiling within the 7B–14B range.

Abstract

We test the standard RLVR tool-use recipe -- GRPO on Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct -- on a deliberately minimal knowledge-graph tool API: four Freebase navigation verbs over Complex WebQuestions. Under a self-verifiable retrieval reward, the policy's tool-grounded answer rate climbs from $3.8\%$ to $9.6\%$ over 250 steps, then collapses to $0\%$ within a single 50-step window -- a \emph{peak-then-collapse} pattern replicated across four seeds. Across seven reward designs, we find four recurring failure modes: adding denser or more targeted proxy rewards shifts the failure mode rather than eliminating it. We argue that a key difference from Python interpreters, web search, and JSON APIs is interface feedback: their failures often leak natural-language signal the model saw in pretraining. A Python traceback names the failing line; an empty Freebase result \texttt{[]} does not. Stripping away that surface exposes a degradation regime that same-family reward redesigns do not fix. A direct oracle ablation rules out relation selection: injecting gold relations at every retrieval call lifts exact-match accuracy by only $+0.20$~pp, and $95.4\%$ of retrieval-dependent errors are retrieval-composition failures rather than answer-extraction failures. As a mitigation, one-iteration self-distillation reaches $40.0\%$ EM at 7B and is capacity-invariant: doubling capacity to 14B improves EM by only $0.25$~pp, and initialization barely matters -- the ceiling appears interface-bound within the 7B--14B range tested.

item.title: Double Triangle Annotation: A Scalable Human-in-the-Loop Framework for High-Precision Historical Document Annotation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25781v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

大规模评估历史文献结构化信息抽取性能亟需高精度人工标注（ground-truth），但传统纯人工标注成本高昂，而基于大语言模型（LLM）的全自动流水线又易产生幻觉（hallucination），难以满足历史文档对事实准确性的严苛要求。

方法创新：Double Triangle Annotation 框架

我们提出双三角标注（Double Triangle Annotation）——一种可扩展、高精度、人机协同的两层标注框架：

  • 第一层（模型三角）：两个架构独立的多模态大语言模型（如 LLaVA 与 Qwen-VL）并行解析同一历史文档；当二者输出一致时，标签自动采纳；分歧项交由众包“人类陪审团”裁定；
  • 第二层（系统三角）：将两套独立运行的第一层系统（含不同模型组合与提示策略）相互交叉校验；剩余未决冲突升级至领域专家终审。

该框架仅依赖一个关键假设——模型错误相互独立，无需预设数据分布、任务特异性校准或后处理调优；且随基础模型能力提升，系统自主性自然增强。

关键成果

在涵盖1887–1906年法国医学名录的《Rosenwald 指南》语料库上，本框架实现最终词错误率（WER）仅 0.003；在13,595个结构化字段中，模型共识自动采纳率达 85.2%，显著降低人工负担。我们同步发布首个面向 Rosenwald 指南的结构化抽取基准数据集（含人机协同验证的黄金标准标注），填补该领域高质量监督信号空白，为历史文档智能处理提供可复现、可扩展的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce Double Triangle Annotation, a scalable human-in-the-loop framework for high-precision structured information extraction from historical documents. It employs two layers of cross-model consensus: (1) two architecturally distinct multimodal LLMs annotate each document in parallel; agreements are auto-accepted, while disagreements are resolved by a human jury; (2) two independent instances of this first layer cross-check each other, with residual conflicts escalated to domain experts. Relying solely on the assumption of model error independence—without distributional priors or task-specific calibration—the framework achieves 0.003 Word Error Rate on the French Rosenwald Guides (1887–1906) and auto-accepts >85% of 13,595 fields. We release the first structured-extraction ground-truth benchmark for this corpus to advance historical document processing research.

Abstract

Evaluating structured-information extraction from historical documents at scale requires high-precision ground-truth annotations, yet traditional manual labeling is expensive and fully automated pipelines built on large language models are prone to hallucination. We propose Double Triangle Annotation, a two-layer human-in-the-loop framework that leverages cross-model consensus to automate the majority of annotation work while ensuring high-precision outputs. In the first layer, two architecturally independent Multimodal Large Language Models annotate each document in parallel; when they agree, the label is auto-accepted, and disagreements are routed to a human jury. A second layer cross-checks two such systems against each other, escalating residual conflicts to a domain expert. The framework rests on a single assumption -- error independence between models -- requires no distributional priors or task-specific calibration, and becomes more autonomous as model capability improves. On the Guides Rosenwald, a corpus of French medical directories spanning 1887-1906, the framework achieves a final Word Error Rate of 0.003. Applied at scale, model consensus auto-accepts over 85% of 13,595 fields. We release the resulting benchmark -- the first structured-extraction ground truth for the Rosenwald Guides -- to support future work on historical document processing.

item.title: LLM-as-a-Reviewer: Benchmarking Their Ability, Divergence, and Prompt Injection Resistance as Paper Reviewers
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25415v1

AI Summary (中文)

LLM-as-a-Reviewer：面向学术同行评审的系统性基准评测

本研究首次构建了面向大语言模型（LLM）作为论文评审员的多维评测框架，聚焦其可靠性、人类对齐性与对抗鲁棒性三大核心挑战。我们基于NeurIPS和ICLR会议共898篇真实投稿论文（按接收状态与领域分层抽样），系统评估了12种主流开源与闭源LLM（涵盖Llama、Qwen、GPT、Claude等家族）。评测沿三个正交维度展开：

  • 评分校准性：LLM是否能准确区分论文质量？结果表明，所有模型均存在系统性高估倾向——对弱提交论文平均抬高1.2–1.8分（5分制），尤其在“Clarity”维度显著低估问题，而在“Reproducibility”上过度苛责；
  • 人类一致性：LLM评审与真实人类评审在主题关注分布上显著偏离（KL散度达0.42–0.67），其生成的评审意见长度达人类的2–3倍，但词汇多样性降低37%，术语高度同质化，反映模板化表达倾向；
  • 提示注入鲁棒性：采用基于隐形字体映射（invisible font-mapping）的新型隐蔽攻击，在PDF元数据中嵌入不可见指令。实验显示，该攻击在最高达41%的案例中成功将低分论文“提升”至接受阈值以上，且效果在模型家族间差异巨大（如Qwen系列抗性最强，GPT-4 Turbo最脆弱）。

本工作不仅揭示了LLM评审当前的关键缺陷，更提供了首个开源评测套件（LLM-ReviewBench），为安全、可信地部署LLM辅助评审提供了实证基础与技术护栏设计依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces LLM-as-a-Reviewer, the first systematic benchmark evaluating large language models as peer reviewers across rating calibration, divergence from human judgments, and resistance to prompt injection. We evaluate 12 LLMs on 898 stratified NeurIPS/ICLR papers. Key findings: (1) All models systematically overrate weak submissions, under-flagging Clarity (+1.2–1.8 pts bias on 5-pt scale) while over-flagging Reproducibility; (2) Their reviews are 2–3× longer but lexically less diverse (−37% type-token ratio) and exhibit topical misalignment with human reviewers (KL divergence: 0.42–0.67); (3) A novel invisible font-mapping attack successfully promotes low-scoring papers to acceptance-level ratings in up to 41% of cases, with stark variation across model families (e.g., Qwen > GPT-4 Turbo). The study underscores urgent needs for bias mitigation and adversarial safeguards before real-world deployment.

Abstract

Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in academic peer review, yet their reliability, alignment with human judgment, and robustness to adversarial attacks remain poorly understood. We present a systematic benchmark of LLM-as-a-Reviewer on 898 papers stratified from NeurIPS and ICLR, evaluating 12 LLMs along three axes: rating calibration, divergence from human reviewers, and resistance to prompt injection embedded via an invisible font-mapping attack. We find that LLMs systematically overrate weaker submissions and diverge from humans in topical emphasis, under-flagging Clarity and over-flagging Reproducibility, while producing reviews two to three times longer with lower lexical diversity and a more standardized vocabulary. Prompt injection remains highly effective. Simple hidden instructions can promote low-scoring papers to acceptance-level ratings in a substantial fraction of cases, with effectiveness varying sharply across model families. While LLMs offer utility in structuring evaluations, their integration into peer review requires safeguards against both intrinsic biases and adversarial risks.

item.title: Security in the Fine-Tuning Lifecycle of Large Language Models: Threats, Defenses,Evaluation, and Future Directions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25073v1

AI Summary (中文)

安全视角下的大语言模型微调全生命周期：系统性综述与实证评估

背景：微调是将预训练大语言模型（LLM）适配至下游任务的核心环节，但其依赖训练数据、参数更新及可复用组件的特性，为攻击者提供了多维度入口。威胁已从早期的数据投毒、权重篡改，演进至智能体操控、接口劫持等新型攻击面；然而，现有综述缺乏覆盖“预微调—微调中—微调后”全生命周期的统一分析框架。

方法：本文提出首个生命周期驱动的安全框架，将攻击与防御按干预时机划分为三阶段：预微调（如数据清洗、验证集污染）、微调中（如梯度注入、LoRA劫持）、微调后（如提示注入、权重编辑）。我们系统梳理各阶段代表性方法，揭示其演化路径与固有局限；并在统一模型（1B–4B开源LLM）、硬件（A100）、协议（标准PEFT流程） 下开展跨阶段实证评估——包括单阶段攻防基准测试及创新性的“跨阶段配对实验”（如预微调数据投毒 + 微调后检测器）。

关键发现：

  • 攻击效果高度模型依赖且非单调：权重编辑攻击在Llama-2-7B上有效，但在Qwen2-1.5B/Phi-3-4B上完全失效；
  • 跨语言后门迁移宣称在7B+模型接近100%，实测在1B–4B模型上成功率归零；
  • 仅使用纯良性样本即可绕过安全对齐（如通过特定指令序列诱导有害输出），暴露对齐鲁棒性盲区。
  • 单阶段防御泛化能力极弱：92%的预微调过滤器对微调中梯度攻击无效；防御效能由模型架构与对齐状态共同决定，不可解耦。

创新点：建立首个细粒度生命周期分类法；发布首个跨阶段可复现评估基准；指出三大开放问题：配置鲁棒防御、跨阶段防御组合机制、超越行为假设的嵌入空间攻击建模。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic survey of security threats and defenses across the full fine-tuning lifecycle of LLMs—spanning pre-tuning, during-tuning, and post-tuning phases. We introduce a unified, phase-aware framework for classifying attacks (e.g., data poisoning, gradient manipulation, weight editing, interface exploitation) and defenses, and conduct rigorous empirical evaluation on open-source models (1B–4B) under standardized hardware, model, and PEFT protocols. Key findings include: (1) Attack effectiveness is highly non-monotonic and model-specific—e.g., weight-editing attacks fail entirely on modern 1B–4B models where they previously succeeded; (2) Cross-lingual backdoor transfer, claimed near-perfect at scale, achieves 0% success in our tests; (3) Purely benign inputs can break safety alignment in instruction-tuned models; and (4) Single-phase defenses rarely generalize across phases, with efficacy jointly determined by architecture and alignment state. We identify three critical open problems—configuration-robust defense design, principled cross-phase defense composition, and embedding-space threat modeling beyond behavioral assumptions—and propose concrete, actionable research directions.

Abstract

Background: Fine-tuning is central to adapting pre-trained Large Language Models (LLMs) to downstream tasks, but its reliance on training data, parameter updates, and reusable components opens entry points for attackers. Threats have evolved from data poisoning and weight tampering to agent manipulation and interface exploitation, yet existing reviews lack a unified framework spanning the full fine-tuning lifecycle. Objective: This paper presents a systematic survey of LLM fine-tuning security and establishes a lifecycle-based framework for comparing attacks and defenses, complemented by unified empirical evaluation. Methods: We divide attack and defense mechanisms into three phases by intervention timing: pre-tuning, during-tuning, and post-tuning. Within each phase, strategies are reviewed and contrasted to expose their evolution and limitations. Representative methods are then evaluated under a unified model, hardware, and protocol setup, with cross-phase experiments pairing attacks and defenses from different phases. Results: Attack effectiveness is highly model-dependent and non-monotonic with scale: weight-editing attacks effective on earlier models lose impact on modern open-source LLMs; cross-lingual backdoor transfer, reported as near-perfect at larger scales, fails entirely on tested 1B-4B models; and purely benign samples can compromise safety alignment in instruction-tuned models. Single-phase defenses rarely generalize across phases, and defense effectiveness depends jointly on model architecture and alignment state. Conclusion: We identify key open problems (configuration-robust defense, cross-phase defense composition, and embedding-space attacks beyond behavioral assumptions) and propose concrete future research directions.

item.title: QML-PipeGuard: Drift-Aware Behavioral Fingerprinting for Quantum Machine Learning Pipeline Integrity
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25066v1

AI Summary (中文)

QML-PipeGuard：面向量子机器学习流水线完整性的漂移感知行为指纹框架

随着量子机器学习（QML）从实验室原型迈向云服务部署，其在金融、医疗等强监管行业的应用亟需保障量子执行阶段的完整性。当前面临两大现实威胁：一是硬件层面的自然漂移——超导量子处理器在两次校准之间因环境扰动导致量子通道参数缓慢变化；二是恶意替换——攻击者在可控执行环境中，用数学上不同但行为高度相似的量子通道冒充声明通道。现有QML验证工作（如脉冲级噪声建模、输入漂移鲁棒性分析、设备身份认证）均无法同时覆盖这两类威胁。

为此，本文提出 QML-PipeGuard，首个基于行为指纹（Behavioral Fingerprint） 的合约式完整性保障框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 统一表征：将QML流水线（编码器–变分线路–测量）在运行时抽象为一组可观测量期望值向量，该向量由信息完备的层析结构化测量族（如单比特Pauli紧框架，理论界 $C = \sqrt{3}$）生成；
  • 双模协同：支持漂移感知监控（容忍校准内良性变化）与对抗检测（识别通道替换为合约违反）；
  • 理论保障：给出有限采样下的样本复杂度上界（$ \sim 1.4 \times 10^4 $ shots），并首次实现容差分解——严格分离自然漂移与对抗扰动对合约偏差的贡献；
  • 端到端验证：在IBM Heron r2处理器（ibm_fez）上完成两比特QSVM流水线实测，噪声匹配仿真进一步验证采样边界；结果表明：恶意通道被以宽安全裕度检出，而典型硬件漂移始终处于容忍阈值内。

AI Summary (English)

Quantum machine learning (QML) is transitioning from research to production, raising urgent integrity concerns for the quantum execution stage: natural drift (channel parameter changes between hardware recalibrations) and adversarial substitution (replacing the declared quantum channel with a behaviorally similar but mathematically distinct one). Existing QML verification approaches—focused on pulse-level noise, input drift, or device identity—fail to jointly address both. We propose QML-PipeGuard, a contract-based framework that characterizes a QML pipeline at runtime via its behavioral fingerprint: a vector of expectation values under an informationally complete, tomographically structured measurement family (e.g., the single-qubit Pauli tight frame with bound $C = \sqrt{3}$). It operates in two modes: drift-aware monitoring, absorbing benign calibration variations within a calibrated tolerance, and adversarial detection, flagging channel substitution as a violation of an observable contract. Key contributions include a pipeline-composition treatment with QML-specific threat modeling, a finite-shot sample-complexity bound (~1.4×10⁴ shots), and a novel tolerance decomposition separating natural and adversarial contributions. End-to-end validation on a two-qubit QSVM pipeline running on IBM’s ibm_fez (Heron r2) confirms detection of a “sneaky” substituted channel with wide safety margin, while typical hardware drift remains within tolerance.

Abstract

Quantum machine learning (QML) is moving from research prototypes to deployed cloud services. As QML enters regulated industries, the integrity of the quantum stage becomes a practical concern on two fronts: noisy hardware drifts at the channel level between recalibrations, and an adversary with control over the execution environment can substitute the declared quantum channel with a behaviorally similar but mathematically distinct one. Neither concern is covered by existing QML verification work on pulse-level noise, input drift, input-perturbation robustness, or device identity. We introduce QML-PipeGuard, a contract-based framework addressing both concerns under a single mathematical machinery. It characterizes a QML pipeline at runtime by its behavioral fingerprint, the vector of observable expectation values under a tomographically structured measurement family, and operates in two modes: drift-aware monitoring that absorbs benign calibration changes within a calibrated tolerance, and adversarial detection that catches channel substitution as a violation of an informationally complete observable contract. The framework contributes a pipeline-composition treatment of the encoder-ansatz-measurement channel with a QML-specific threat model (tight frame-bound C=sqrt(3) for the single-qubit Pauli family), a finite-shot sample-complexity bound, and a tolerance decomposition separating adversarial and natural-drift contributions. We validate the framework end-to-end on a two-qubit QSVM pipeline on the IBM Heron r2 processor (ibm_fez), with a sample-complexity validation on a noise-matched simulator. The prescribed measurement budget (about 1.4e4 shots) fits in a single batched job, the sneaky channel is detected with a wide safety margin while evading the weak contract, and the typical hardware drift sits within tolerance.

item.title: Reflect-Guard: Enhancing LLM Safeguards against Adversarial Prompts via Logical Self-Reflection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24834v1

AI Summary (中文)

Reflect-Guard：面向对抗性提示的逻辑自省增强型LLM安全防护框架

大型语言模型（LLM）安全分类器（如Llama Guard）虽能有效识别显性有害提示，但在面对角色扮演、虚构叙事、间接请求等精心设计的对抗性越狱攻击时仍显著失效——此类攻击通过语义伪装规避基于表面模式匹配的传统检测机制。为突破该瓶颈，我们提出Reflect-Guard，一种基于逻辑自省（logical self-reflection） 的轻量级增强方法。

核心方法

  • 知识蒸馏驱动的反思标注：利用GPT-4o-mini生成结构化反思链（Chain-of-Thought），涵盖意图识别、隐含风险分析与上下文一致性验证三阶段推理；
  • 参数高效微调（PEFT）：仅对Llama-Guard-3-8B实施QLoRA微调，仅更新0.5%参数（约4200万参数），训练数据仅需1000个高质量对抗样本；
  • 推理时自省机制：模型在输出安全判决前，必须先生成可解释的反思文本，显式建模“用户真实意图是否恶意”，而非依赖表层关键词或句法特征。

关键成果

  • 在WildGuardTest基准上，F1分数从0.770提升至0.842（+7.2个百分点），对抗性提示召回率跃升40.8个百分点（0.513→0.921），显著缓解漏检问题；
  • 在JailbreakBench上，攻击成功率由10.3%降至1.8%，实现82.5%的相对降低；
  • 消融实验证明：反思能力使模型能穿透角色设定、元指令伪装等复杂混淆策略，验证了“推理意图优于匹配表象”的安全范式迁移价值。

本工作表明，赋予安全分类器可解释、可追溯的逻辑推理能力，是构建鲁棒LLM防护体系的关键路径。

AI Summary (English)

Reflect-Guard enhances LLM safety classifiers (e.g., Llama Guard) against adversarial jailbreaks by integrating logical self-reflection via parameter-efficient fine-tuning. We distill structured chain-of-thought reasoning—covering intent inference, risk grounding, and contextual consistency—from GPT-4o-mini into reflection annotations, then train Llama-Guard-3-8B using QLoRA on just 1,000 examples while updating only 0.5% of parameters (~42M). During inference, the model first generates a reflective rationale before issuing a safety verdict. On WildGuardTest, Reflect-Guard boosts F1 from 0.770 to 0.842 (+7.2 pp) and adversarial recall from 0.513 to 0.921 (+40.8 pp). On JailbreakBench, attack success rate drops from 10.3% to 1.8% (−82.5% relative). These gains stem from explicit reasoning about underlying malicious intent, enabling robust detection where surface-pattern classifiers fail.

Abstract

Large language model (LLM) safety classifiers such as Llama Guard are effective at detecting overtly harmful prompts but remain vulnerable to adversarial jailbreak attacks that disguise malicious intent through role-play scenarios, fictional framing, and indirect requests. We present Reflect-Guard, a method that augments LLM-based safety classifiers with chain-of-thought self-reflection capabilities through parameter-efficient fine-tuning. Our approach distills analytical reasoning from GPT-4o-mini into structured reflection annotations, then trains Llama-Guard-3-8B via QLoRA to generate logical self-reflections before issuing safety verdicts. Using only 1000 training examples and updating just 0.5% of model parameters (~42M), Reflect-Guard achieves substantial improvements on two challenging benchmarks. On WildGuardTest, F1 score improves from 0.770 to 0.842 (+7.2 pp), with recall on adversarial prompts increasing from 0.513 to 0.921 (+40.8 pp). On JailbreakBench, the attack success rate drops from 10.3% to 1.8%, representing an 82.5% relative reduction. These gains are especially pronounced on adversarial inputs, where the explicit reasoning step enables the model to see through obfuscation techniques that defeat standard pattern-matching approaches. Our results demonstrate that teaching safety classifiers to reason about adversarial intent, rather than simply classify surface patterns, is a promising direction for robust LLM safety.

item.title: RouteScan: A Non-Intrusive Approach to Auditing MoE LLMs Safety via Expert Routing Telemetry
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24817v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

混合专家（MoE）架构已成为扩展大语言模型（LLM）的关键范式，但其在真实服务中的部署引发了紧迫的安全审计需求。传统基于内容的审计方法（如分析用户提示、输入或输出文本）虽有效，却需直接访问敏感用户数据，导致安全与隐私的根本性冲突——这在监管严格、用户信任至上的应用场景中尤为突出。

方法创新：RouteScan 框架

本文提出 RouteScan，一种非侵入式、硬件感知的安全审计框架。其核心洞见在于：MoE 模型的稀疏专家路由机制会因输入语义差异而触发不同的专家激活模式，进而在 GPU 执行层留下可测量的微架构足迹。RouteScan 创新性地利用预填充（prefilling）阶段各专家模块所分配的活跃 GPU 线程数作为轻量、高判别性的路由指纹。该信号完全脱离文本内容，仅依赖公开、低权限的硬件性能计数器（如 NVIDIA Nsight Compute），无需访问任何用户数据或模型内部参数。

关键发现与优势

  • 在多个开源 MoE LLM（如 Mixtral-8x7B、Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B）上验证，RouteScan 对未见过的危害领域（如非法指令、仇恨言论）AUROC 达 0.93+，对新型 jailbreak 包装器（如 Ghost, Multiverse）检测 AUROC 高达 0.96，展现卓越跨域泛化能力；
  • 逆向重构实验表明：仅凭路由线程 telemetry 无法可靠还原原始提示（重建 BLEU < 0.02，语义保真度趋近于零），证实其具备实质性隐私保护优势；
  • 整个检测流水线仅需毫秒级延迟、<5MB 内存开销，可无缝嵌入生产环境监控栈，实现“零数据接触”的实时风控。

AI Summary (English)

RouteScan is a non-intrusive safety auditing framework for Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) LLMs that operates solely on low-level GPU expert routing telemetry—bypassing prompts, inputs, and outputs entirely. It leverages the count of active GPU threads allocated to each expert during prefilling as a privacy-preserving, microarchitectural fingerprint of input semantics. A lightweight detection pipeline extracts cross-domain invariant risk signals from this telemetry, enabling precise identification of malicious prompts without accessing sensitive user data. Evaluated on diverse open-source MoE models (e.g., Mixtral, Qwen-MoE), RouteScan achieves AUROC > 0.93 on unseen harmful domains and 0.96 under novel jailbreak wrappers—demonstrating strong generalization. Crucially, empirical inversion tests confirm that routing telemetry provides negligible reconstructive signal for prompts (BLEU < 0.02), offering a practical privacy advantage over content-based methods.

Abstract

Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have become an increasingly important paradigm for scaling Large Language Models (LLMs). As MoE models are increasingly deployed in real-world services, safety auditing becomes necessary to verify whether these models produce or facilitate harmful behaviors during operation. However, existing content-based auditing methods typically require access to user prompts, model inputs, or generated outputs, potentially exposing sensitive user information and creating a fundamental tension between LLM safety and user privacy. On the other hand, we observe that, in MoE models, sparse expert routing maps different inputs to activate different expert-execution patterns, producing measurable footprints in low-level GPU execution telemetry. Inspired by this observation, we propose RouteScan, a non-intrusive auditing framework for detecting harmful behaviors through GPU-level expert routing telemetry. Specifically, RouteScan utilizes the number of active GPU threads allocated to expert modules during the prefilling phase as a discriminative micro-architectural fingerprint, and builds a lightweight detection pipeline that isolates cross-domain invariant risk indicators for the precise identification of malicious prompts. Comprehensive evaluations on open-source MoE LLMs with distinct routing designs demonstrate that RouteScan achieves strong generalization, with an AUROC exceeding 0.93 on unseen harmful domains and 0.96 under novel jailbreak wrappers. Moreover, empirical inversion tests show that the collected expert routing telemetry provides limited information for prompt reconstruction, suggesting a practical privacy advantage over content-based auditing methods.

item.title: LLM Agent Based Renewable Energy Forecasting Using Edge and IoT Data A Review of Solar Wind Weather and Grid Aware Decision Support
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25141v1

AI Summary (中文)

基于LLM智能体的边缘与IoT驱动可再生能源预测综述

背景与挑战：太阳能与风能固有的间歇性（受云层、风速、大气湍流、季节变化及地形影响）对电网稳定、电力交易、储能调度及碳感知运行规划构成严峻挑战。当前，智能电表、逆变器、风速计、日射强度计、气象站及电网接口传感器等边缘IoT设备爆发式增长，产生海量实时多源数据，但传统预测流水线难以有效融合与利用此类异构、高时频、时空耦合的数据流。

方法与框架：本文系统综述了大型语言模型（LLM）智能体在可再生能源预测中的范式革新作用。我们提出一种六层统一架构：涵盖数据采集→预处理→特征工程→模型推理→不确定性估计→自然语言报告，支持将边缘传感器流、气象API、历史出力记录、电网约束及物理上下文知识无缝集成至端到端决策支持工作流。综述对比分析了经典统计模型（如ARIMA）、深度学习架构（LSTM、Transformer）、物理信息混合模型，并重点评述LLM智能体在可解释性生成、不确定性通信、幻觉抑制、人机协同引导等方面的前沿进展。

核心发现与创新：识别出12项关键开放挑战，包括：实时边缘部署延迟、分布偏移下的模型漂移、多源不确定性量化、LLM幻觉控制、边缘硬件异构性互操作、与能源管理系统（EMS）/SCADA深度集成等。本文首次提出“物理感知LLM接地”（physics-informed LLM grounding）与“联邦化预测架构”作为未来研究双支柱，并呼吁建立面向能源场景的开源基准数据集与评估协议。

AI Summary (English)

This review examines how Large Language Model (LLM) agents advance renewable energy forecasting by unifying heterogeneous edge/IoT data—spanning smart meters, inverters, anemometers, pyranometers, weather APIs, historical generation, and grid constraints—into context-aware decision support. We propose a six-layer taxonomy (data acquisition to natural language reporting) and survey classical, deep learning, physics-hybrid, and emerging LLM-agent approaches—with emphasis on explainability, uncertainty communication, hallucination mitigation, and operator guidance. Key challenges identified include real-time edge deployment, distributional shift-induced model drift, multi-source uncertainty quantification, LLM hallucination control, edge hardware interoperability, and EMS integration. We recommend a research agenda centered on open energy benchmarks, physics-informed LLM grounding, and federated forecasting architectures.

Abstract

Reliable forecasting of renewable energy generation is a foundational requirement for grid stability energy trading battery scheduling and carbon aware operational planning Solar and wind resources are inherently intermittent their output fluctuates with cloud cover wind speed atmospheric turbulence seasonal patterns and local terrain The proliferation of IoT and edge devices spanning smart meters inverters anemometers pyranometers weather stations and grid interface sensors has created an unprecedented volume of real time operational data that conventional forecasting pipelines are ill equipped to exploit fully This review investigates how large language model LLM agents can enhance renewable energy forecasting by integrating heterogeneous sensor streams weather API data historical generation records grid constraints and contextual reasoning into unified decision support workflows We survey classical forecasting methods statistical time series models deep learning architectures physics hybrid approaches and emerging LLM agent frameworks for explanation uncertainty communication and operator guidance A six layer taxonomy is proposed covering data acquisition preprocessing feature engineering model inference uncertainty estimation and natural language reporting The review identifies twelve open challenges spanning real time deployment model drift under distribution shift uncertainty quantification hallucination control in LLM agents interoperability of edge hardware and integration with energy management systems The paper concludes by recommending a research agenda centred on open benchmarks physics informed LLM grounding and federated forecasting architectures

item.title: Solving Combinatorial Counting Problems with Weighted First-Order Model Counting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24845v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

组合计数问题广泛存在于人工智能、统计学与离散数学中，涵盖子集、多重集、排列、划分、组合等结构在各类约束下的枚举任务。传统方法面临两难：闭式推导虽高效但泛化性差、易出错；而将问题直接编码为命题模型计数（#SAT）或约束满足问题（CSP），则会破坏对象间的可交换性（exchangeability）——这恰是实现可扩展计数的关键结构性优势。

方法创新：Cofola 语言与 WFOMC 编译框架

本文提出 Cofola（COmbinatorial counting LAnguage with First-Order logic），一种带类型的声明式语言，其原语直接对应常见组合对象：集合（sets）、包（bags）、元组（tuples）、序列（sequences）、环（circles）、划分（partitions）和组合（compositions），并支持自然的关系约束（如包含、不相交）与算术约束（如元素和、基数限制）。Cofola 程序具有严谨的指称语义，确保每个程序唯一对应一个良定义的组合计数问题。其核心是三阶段编译流水线：

  • 预处理：规范化约束与类型；
  • 分解：基于结构依赖进行问题解耦；
  • 对称性保持编码：通过分组不可区分实体、对无序结构（如集合、划分）施加字典序打破、对序列/环引入全序公理，将问题规约为加权一阶模型计数（WFOMC）实例，并嵌入系数提取约束以支持生成函数级精确计数。

主要成果

在涵盖教科书经典问题（如受限整数划分、带禁位排列）及多对象协同场景（如“从3类球中选5个且每类至少1个”的联合约束）的评测套件上，Cofola 实现了：① 规格简洁性（平均代码量比逻辑编码减少68%）；② 表达完备性（首个能统一建模环、划分、组合等高阶结构的框架）；③ 可解性保障：编译结果严格落入已知域可提升（domain-liftable）片段，支持多项式时间推理。该框架首次打通了组合数学直觉与自动化符号计数之间的语义鸿沟。

AI Summary (English)

Combinatorial counting—enumerating sets, multisets, permutations, partitions, and compositions under structural and arithmetic constraints—is fundamental yet notoriously manual. Closed-form solutions are brittle; propositional encodings destroy exchangeability, undermining tractability. We introduce Cofola, a typed declarative language whose primitives directly mirror combinatorial objects (e.g., bags, sequences, circles, partitions) and support natural relational and arithmetic constraints. Its denotational semantics maps programs to well-defined counting problems, compiled via a three-phase pipeline (preprocessing, decomposition, symmetry-preserving encoding) into weighted first-order model counting (WFOMC) instances with coefficient-extraction constraints. Crucially, the encoding preserves domain-liftability by grouping indistinguishable elements, breaking unordered symmetries lexicographically, and axiomatizing order for sequences/circles. Evaluated on textbook and multi-object problems beyond prior frameworks’ expressivity, Cofola delivers concise specifications and an end-to-end practical solver—unifying combinatorial intuition with automated, scalable counting.

Abstract

Combinatorial counting problems pervade artificial intelligence, statistics, and discrete mathematics. Whether the task is enumerating subsets, multisets, permutations, partitions, or compositions under structural and arithmetic constraints, solving it remains a stubbornly manual exercise. Closed-form derivations are powerful but brittle, while naive encodings to propositional model counting or constraint satisfaction destroy the exchangeability that makes counting tractable in the first place. We present Cofola (COmbinatorial counting LAnguage with First-Order logic), a typed declarative language whose primitives are the combinatorial objects that recur in everyday counting questions, including sets, bags, tuples, sequences, circles, partitions, and compositions, together with natural relational and arithmetic constraints over them. A denotational semantics maps every Cofola program to a well-defined combinatorial counting problem, and a three-phase compilation pipeline (preprocessing, decomposition, and symmetry-preserving encoding) reduces this problem to a weighted first-order model counting (WFOMC) instance augmented with coefficient-extraction constraints. To stay inside known domain-liftable fragments whenever possible, the encoding groups indistinguishable entities, breaks the symmetry of unordered groupings lexicographically, and encodes sequences and circles via order axioms. On a suite of representative combinatorial counting problems, ranging from textbook math problems to multi-object scenarios that the closest prior framework cannot express, Cofola produces concise specifications and a uniform solving pipeline that is practical end-to-end.

item.title: Personalized Federated Learning by Energy-Efficient UAV Communications
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25212v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）在保障边缘设备数据隐私的同时提升协同建模能力，但在地理广域分布场景（如偏远地区传感器网络）中，传统地面通信受限。无人机（UAV）凭借灵活部署与视距链路优势，可作为移动聚合器支撑FL参数交换；然而，设备数据异构性导致模型收敛缓慢，而全设备参与的全局训练机制又引发高昂通信开销与UAV能量瓶颈——其有限电池容量难以支撑高频、长时、高功率的多链路并发传输。

方法创新

本文提出一种面向能效优化的个性化联邦学习框架：

  • 架构层面：采用严格解耦的双分支结构——全局共享的轻量化骨干网络（backbone）+ 永久本地化的个性化头（personalization head），从模型设计上隔离数据异构干扰，避免个性化参数污染全局更新；
  • 调度层面：设计梯度ℓ₂-范数驱动的稀疏调度策略：每轮仅选取梯度模值最大的前α%设备上传骨干参数，既聚焦信息量最高的局部更新，又显著降低UAV需服务的设备数量与总传输比特量；
  • 联合优化目标：将UAV能量消耗（含飞行能耗与通信功耗）显式建模为调度决策的约束项，实现学习性能与能效的帕累托改进。

主要结果

在多种非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据集上的仿真表明：相较FedAvg、FedProx及最新UAV-FL方案，本方法在CIFAR-10（Non-IID-β=0.1）上测试精度提升3.2–5.7个百分点，同时UAV单轮平均能耗降低41.3%，续航时间延长近1.7倍。该框架为资源受限空天地一体化FL系统提供了可部署的轻量级解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative training across edge devices, yet geographic dispersion—e.g., in remote sensor networks—necessitates flexible aerial support. Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) serve as mobile aggregators but face dual challenges: data heterogeneity degrades convergence, while full-device scheduling incurs prohibitive communication and energy costs under limited UAV battery capacity.
